message:
fix(node): replace HashMap/HashSet with BTreeMap/BTreeSet for deterministic behavior across 68 modules; fix remote_cap expiry boundary

Non-deterministic iteration order from HashMap/HashSet can cause
inconsistent serialization digests, flaky test assertions, and
unpredictable audit log ordering across runs. This sweep replaces all
HashMap→BTreeMap and HashSet→BTreeSet usages across the connector,
control_plane, security, policy, runtime, federation, encoding,
observability, storage, supply_chain, testing, tools, and verifier_economy
modules, as well as conformance/integration/security test suites and the
cx_first_policy lint tool.

Key changes by module area:
- connector (21 files): canonical_serializer schemas map, control_channel
  replay windows, crdt state/tombstone sets, trust_fabric peer maps,
  conformance_profile required-capability maps, and all uniqueness-check
  test assertions now use ordered collections
- security (8 files): barrier_primitives engine maps, degraded_mode_policy
  permitted/denied action sets and recovery status sets, adversarial_runner
  category/mutation uniqueness checks, sybil_defense peer tracking,
  threshold_sig participant sets, remote_cap consumed/revoked token sets
- remote_cap: also fixes fail-closed expiry boundary — capabilities are
  now rejected at the exact expiry second (>= instead of >) matching
  standard token semantics; adds regression test
  token_is_denied_at_exact_expiry_boundary
- control_plane (3 files): audience_token scope sets, control_epoch
  participant tracking, key_role_separation key maps
- policy (4 files): boundary check registries, correctness envelope
  constraint sets, hardening trigger condition maps, object class tuning
- runtime (2 files): anti_entropy divergence tracking, authority_audit
  role maps
- tests (10 files): all conformance, integration, and security tests
  updated to match new ordered collection types

All PartialOrd+Ord derives added where needed as BTreeMap/BTreeSet keys
(TrustObjectType, Profile, etc.).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
